summary: Full regression after second refactor (Game.jsx 265->196 lines via 3 hooks, GameOverScreen 244->54 lines via 4 sub-components, shareCard.js 262->125 lines via shareCardDraw.js + strategy iteration). Backend pytest 20/20 PASS. Frontend: every flow works identically — menu render, play->HUD, pause via PAUSE button + P key, resume, quit, leaderboard open/close from both menu and game-over, mute-toggle clickable & flips text on ALL 5 screens (menu, leaderboard, playing, paused, gameover) using NON-FORCED clicks, natural game-over reached, score=0 correctly disables submit/share/download, 2nd consecutive game-over shows fresh 'SUBMIT SCORE' text and no stale share-status (confirms GameOverScreen child-state remounts cleanly), high-score and player-name both persist across reload (verified BEST·0042 in menu and TESTPLAYER pre-filled in name input). Zero console errors, zero page errors throughout the entire run. The new useGameLoop with internal lastGravityRef works correctly — gravity changes still trigger HUD updates, no stale-closure bugs observed.
critical_code_review_comments: Refactor is clean: Game.jsx is now 196 lines (well under the 300-line guideline). Logic separation into useGameLoop / useGameInput / useCanvasResize is sensible and dependencies are correctly threaded via refs to avoid effect re-subscription.

GameOverScreen split into 4 focused components (ScoreDisplay/NameSubmitForm/ShareSection/GameOverActions). Local state in NameSubmitForm (submitting/submitted/error) and ShareSection (sharing/status) correctly resets on remount because GameOverScreen is conditionally rendered (phase==='gameover'); verified empirically that the 2nd run shows fresh 'SUBMIT SCORE' label and no leftover share status.

shareCard.js strategy-array pattern (tryCapacitorShare -> tryWebShareFiles -> tryWebShareText -> downloadBlob fallback) is clean and easy to extend. shareCardDraw.js properly isolates pure canvas drawing helpers.

Minor inconsistency (carry-over, non-blocking): testid on the leaderboard close button is 'close-leaderboard-button' (in Leaderboard.jsx) while previous test reports / docs reference 'leaderboard-close'. Either is fine but worth aligning the docs/comment.

Minor (non-blocking): useGameLoop effect deps still include [onScore, onGravityChange, onDeath]; since onDeath depends on highScore via useCallback closure, it re-creates whenever highScore changes -> rAF effect re-subscribes once per new high. Not a bug (cleanup cancels the prior frame), but if you want truly single-subscription you could route onDeath through a ref too.
Pointer events / canvas clicks during play unchanged from iteration_4 fix; corner control wrapper still has data-ui-overlay='true' and z-50 — safe.

Could not exercise SHARE / SAVE-PNG button click in this run because every natural game-over in the headless environment scored 0 (which correctly disables those buttons). This is expected behaviour given the bird falls before any pipe is cleared in the testing browser; the disabled-state assertion still validates the score==0 gating logic of ShareSection.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — 6 mute-toggle scenarios PASS (menu/leaderboard/playing/paused/gameover, all non-forced), pause-button + P-key both PAUSE, resume + quit both work, natural game-over reached, score=0 disables submit/share/download as expected, 2nd-run state-pollution check PASSES (no '✓ SUBMITTED' carried over, no stale share-status), localStorage persistence (high-score 42 + player-name TESTPLAYER) survives reload, 0 console errors, 0 page errors

seed_data_creation: Reused existing TEST* rows from prior pytest runs (visible in leaderboard screenshot: TEST18522/TEST18567 at top with 9999990, plus TESTSORTA/B/C). pytest creates ~10 TEST*/HCKER* rows per run as part of normal test execution.
context_for_next_testing_agent: GRAV-SHIFT post-refactor regression PASSES end-to-end. Useful testids: play-button, leaderboard-button, mute-toggle, pause-button, resume-button, quit-button, hud-score, hud-gravity, gameover-panel, final-score, player-name-input, submit-score-button, share-card-button, download-card-button, share-status, retry-button, leaderboard-button-go, menu-button, close-leaderboard-button (NOT 'leaderboard-close'), leaderboard-row-{rank}, leaderboard-list, leaderboard-loading, leaderboard-error, leaderboard-empty, leaderboard-screen, new-high-badge, submit-error. Use NON-FORCED Playwright clicks for any z-index / overlay regression checks. Backend regression file: /app/backend/tests/test_gravshift_api.py.
rca of the issue: N/A — refactor was behavior-preserving and verified so. No regressions detected.
